summary: Fix: Related files list disappearing when switching sidebar tabs #5789

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.
  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:
  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues
  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

**Description**
Fixes #5789 - Related files list disappears when switching tabs in the sidebar.

## Changes
Changed `v-if` to `v-show` for all sidebar tab components in `MainSidebar.vue` to prevent component destruction and maintain state during tab switching.

**Technical Details**:
- **Root Cause**: `v-if` completely destroys and recreates components, causing the `RelatedFilesTab` component to lose its state
- **Fix**: Using `v-show` only toggles visibility while preserving component instances and their state

**Files Modified**:
- `source/win-main/sidebar/MainSidebar.vue`: Changed 4 instances of `v-if` to `v-show` for tab components

## Additional information
**Testing**:
- [x] Created test files with shared tags and internal links
- [x] Confirmed Related Files tab displays the related files list
- [x] Switched to other tabs and back to Related Files tab
- [x] Verified the list persists and doesn't disappear

**Impact**:
- Only affects sidebar tab switching logic
- No breaking changes to existing functionality
- Improves user experience by maintaining state consistency

**Checklist Notes**:
- Items 4 and 5 (yarn lint/test and CHANGELOG.md) - Need guidance on these
- All other checklist items completed

Tested on: Windows 11

### Description

I can no longer export files containing images due to a Pandoc error (code 64, parsing error). This is odd, as running pandoc from the command line (just `pandoc myfile.md`) works without issue. 

* Using Zettlr's internal Pandoc also results in the error. 
* The issue happens regardless of selected export directory
* All export types fail in this way (html, xelatex, org mode, ...)

Using Zettlr via AppImage also results in the same issues.

Possible related to #5780, but I cannot be sure.

### Reproducing

Not sure. I tried cloning Zettlr and running the development version at tag 3.5.0, and here the issue seemed to be fixed when using internal Pandoc. For external Pandoc, I still got the same issue.



### Zettlr version

3.5.0

### Installation Method

Other (please specify below)

### Installation Method (Other)

Zettlr and AppImage

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Latest Arch Linux

### Additional Information

The error window shows the following:

`An error occurred on export: Pandoc exited with code 64`

```
Error at "/path/to/file.md" (line 12, column 3):
unexpected '6'
![60569583f8023a56f82f54f7065e5e89.png](./Img/60569583f8023a56f82f54f7065e5e89.png)
^
```

For info, my Pandoc version:
```
pandoc 3.1.12.1
Features: +server +lua
Scripting engine: Lua 5.4
```

summary: feat: hide double square brackets in internal links

description.text:
This pull request hides the double square brackets ([[ and ]]) used in internal links in the editor view, improving visual clarity.
If ZknLink AST nodes are available, the plugin hides the pipe (|) and internal target.
As a fallback, it uses a RegExp to hide brackets manually when no AST is available.
This resolves [#5783](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/5783).
I'm happy to receive feedback and make any changes as needed.

summary: Feat/transform quotes context menu ,Text transform: Paired quotes to double quotes,Text transform: Paired quotes to double quotes.

description.text:
Description
This pull request adds new context menu items to the Markdown editor to transform paired quotes. The features allow users to:
- Update 'Automate common text transforms. #5659
 - Update Text transform: Paired quotes to double quotes. #5680
 - Update Text transform: Straighten quotes. #5675
Convert smart quotes (“”, ‘’) into plain quotes (" and ')

Convert paired quotes like ```` and '' to "

Convert single quotes ' to double quotes "

These functions improve editing experience for users who deal with copy-pasted or auto-formatted text.
changed files：
- changelog.md
- source/common/modules/markdown-editor/commands/markdown.ts
- source/common/modules/markdown-editor/context-menu/default-menu.ts

summary: Les modifications que j'avais faites ces deux dernières semaines se sont supprimées

description.text:
### Description

Il y a quelques soirs, j'étais en train de continuer à prendre mes notes sur Zettler, puis j'ai voulu faire une pause dans mon travail et ainsi, j'ai voulu enregistrer malgré tout au cas où ma progression en cliquant deux fois dans le menu de "Fichier" en haut à gauche sur Enregistrer ou Ctrl + S pour sauvegarder le travail. Or en voulant le faire, un rectangle m'est apparu où il est mentionné que c'est impossible d'enregistrer les dernières modifications, avec dedans un bouton rouge "ok". Ne sachant pas quoi faire et me disant que ça allait simplement me fermer cette petite fenêtre, je clique sur "ok" (il me semble, je ne me rappelle pas très bien), et d'un coup les modifications que j'avais faites ces deux dernières semaines (alors que je les avais sauvegardées au préalable sur Zettler, vu qu'avant ça marchait), ont toutes disparu, ce qui m'a beaucoup étonnée. J'imagine qu'il y a double bug de la part de Zettlr, pour avoir effacé plus qu'attendu et pour n'avoir d'un coup pas permis la sauvegarde. Quoi qu'il en soit, j'aimerais retrouver toutes mes notes prises durant ce mois-ci (mai), j'étais en train de faire un gros travail de pagination pour mon mémoire (c'est-à-dire de retrouver les numéro de page des citations piochées dans un livre, ce qui est très long...). Merci beaucoup d'avance pour votre réponse. Mon fichier était essentiellement constitué de notes et de citations... Dois-je vous l'envoyer en PJ ?
Bien cordialement 

### Reproducing

Je pense que ça s'est produit après avoir écrit vite, j'avais le fichier Zettlr sur clé donc si la clé a bougé cela a pu provoquer ce désagrément. Il faut aller sur "Enregistrer" dans le menu "Fichier" en haut à gauche, en cliquant deux fois par contre, parce qu'une seule fois ne suffit pas apparemment. Normalement cela sauve automatiquement les modifications ainsi, mais là ça m'a étrangement supprimé les modifications. 

### Zettlr version

3.4.3

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 10

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Selected word & character count overflows toolbar in narrow windows

description.text:
### Description

In the toolbar, the line wrapping on the selection word count indicator overflows the top and bottom of the toolbar, when the Zettlr window is too narrow and the character count numbers are too large.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0c7508db-ca8c-4fdd-9b35-4a818acbed1e)

### Reproducing

Open a document in Zettlr. Add a couple thousand characters to the document. Select more than 100 words from middle of the document, so that the first selected character and the last selected character are at least the 100th on their line. In the top toolbar, look at the selected word/character count indicator. If the "xxx selected x:xxx - x:xxx" isn't overflowing, narrow the window until it is (about 900px width).

### Zettlr version

3.4.4

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Sequoia 15.5

### Additional Information

My first bug report on Github. I've tried to be clear and comprehensive.

summary: Zettlr File Manager is not showing changes made in a Github folder

description.text:
### Description

I have a Workspace which is also a Github repository (when I open the properties, this is also visible there). When I make any change here (creating a new file, renaming a file, creating a new folder, deleting a file etc). The File Manager doesn't refreshes and shows the previous state. I have this issue on Windows and Mac too. The changes are visible if I open a local folder as a Workspace.

### Reproducing

1. Clone a Github repository locally.
2. Open the folder in Zettlr as a new workspace.
3. Create a new file in the workspace.
4. The new file is not visible in the File Manager.

### Zettlr version

3.4.4

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

Windows 10 & macOS 14.4.1

summary: Zettlr slows down considerably while working on documents over 20k words

description.text:
### Description

When I load a .md document which has over 20k words, typing becomes so slow. I have to wait for a few seconds before the letters appear on the screen. Moving the cursor to another place using mouse also takes a while to appear on the screen. However, a new file (.md document) with zero words works as expected; there is no lag. I tried clearing FSAL cache, but that did not help. The document I am working on has about 20+ citations drawn from a CSL JSON file exported from Zotero.

### Reproducing

1. Open Zettlr
2. Load a local document (.md) with more than 20k words and a few citations.
3. (Optionally, load two or three more documents as this is a usual scenario)
4. Start typing.
5. The typed letters appear in the editor window only after 2-3 seconds.

### Zettlr version

3.4.4 

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Sequoia 15.4.1

### Additional Information

This lag does not happen when writing in documents with smaller word count. It appears once I cross ~15k words or so.
